description:
You are acting as an “artifact-manager” analyst.  
A raw activity log already exists at:  

/home/user/data/artifactory_ops.log  

Your goal is to isolate every *failed* repository operation and then give the DevOps lead a very small report that answers two questions:  

1. Exactly which log-lines failed?  
2. How many failed operations happened per individual repository?  

What you must deliver (and where)
---------------------------------
1. A file named  
   /home/user/reports/failed_ops.log  
   • It must contain **only** the original lines whose status column is the literal word “FAIL”, in the **same order** they appear in the source log.  
   • Do **not** include any header line or your own comments—just the failing log-lines exactly as they were.

2. A second file named  
   /home/user/reports/repo_fail_counts.txt  
   • Each line must have two whitespace-separated fields in this exact layout:  

     <repository_name> <fail_count>  

     Example format (do **not** hard-code these values):  
     repo-alpha 2  
   • The list must be sorted in descending order by the numeric fail_count.  
   • Repositories with the same count can appear in any order relative to each other.  
   • No extra blank lines, headings, or punctuation—just the data lines.

Constraints
-----------
• Use only standard Linux command-line utilities that are available in a minimal distribution.  
• The entire task should be doable with roughly 2–3 shell commands.  
• Ensure /home/user/reports/ exists; create it if it does not.  
• Leave the original log file untouched.  

When you are finished, “failed_ops.log” and “repo_fail_counts.txt” should exist exactly in the locations and formats described above so that an automated test can check them byte-for-byte.
truth:
# Pre-existing environment created by the test harness
Directory: /home/user/data  
File: /home/user/data/artifactory_ops.log  
Exact file contents (11 lines, Unix LF endings):

2023-11-15T10:01:12Z repo-alpha /libs/commons-lang3-3.12.0.jar UPLOAD OK
2023-11-15T10:02:48Z repo-beta /apps/myapp-1.3.1.war DOWNLOAD OK
2023-11-15T10:03:10Z repo-alpha /libs/commons-io-2.11.0.jar UPLOAD FAIL
2023-11-15T10:04:47Z repo-gamma /docker/core-image.tar DELETE OK
2023-11-15T10:05:19Z repo-beta /apps/oldapp-0.9.8.war DELETE FAIL
2023-11-15T10:06:03Z repo-alpha /libs/guava-31.1-jre.jar UPLOAD OK
2023-11-15T10:07:55Z repo-beta /apps/newapp-1.4.0.war DOWNLOAD FAIL
2023-11-15T10:09:12Z repo-gamma /docker/legacy-image.tar UPLOAD OK
2023-11-15T10:10:27Z repo-alpha /libs/log4j-2.17.2.jar DELETE FAIL
2023-11-15T10:11:41Z repo-delta /nuget/packageX.1.0.0.nupkg UPLOAD OK
2023-11-15T10:13:03Z repo-beta /apps/myapp-1.3.2.war DOWNLOAD OK

# After correct agent execution, the following must exist:

1. /home/user/reports/failed_ops.log  
Expected contents (4 lines):

2023-11-15T10:03:10Z repo-alpha /libs/commons-io-2.11.0.jar UPLOAD FAIL
2023-11-15T10:05:19Z repo-beta /apps/oldapp-0.9.8.war DELETE FAIL
2023-11-15T10:07:55Z repo-beta /apps/newapp-1.4.0.war DOWNLOAD FAIL
2023-11-15T10:10:27Z repo-alpha /libs/log4j-2.17.2.jar DELETE FAIL

2. /home/user/reports/repo_fail_counts.txt  
Expected contents (each line ends with LF):

repo-beta 2
repo-alpha 2

# Note: Both files must have 0644 permissions, but the test harness will not fail on mode differences as long as they are readable.
name: task_000000_7f27d6b3
